methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $369M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Locus Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series F), post-money $2B

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Augury, a Series F robotics and manufacturing software company that has raised $369M to date, operates within a sector comprising one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private peers including Locus Robotics, 1X Technologies, and Foundation Robotics. The company faces a constrained peer-benchmarking environment: while 12 same-sector peers exist broadly, the narrow filters of same-stage positioning, adjacent-stage proximity, and comparable valuation tier yielded insufficient datapoints (fewer than three) to support a reliable peer-median methodology.

Consequently, the valuation relies on stage-progression analysis, which applies a 1.3x step-up multiplier to the $369M total capital raised proxy, reflecting typical Series F advancement dynamics. This approach yields an implied valuation of $480M. A conservative sensitivity range—applying ±15% variance around the single-method output—produces a low estimate of $408M and a high estimate of $552M.

The $408M–$552M range, centered on $480M, represents the quantifiable support from available methods. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable-stage peer data to validate or adjust the generic 1.3x multiplier, the representativeness of total raised as a valuation proxy (which conflates deployment efficiency with market value), and limited visibility into Augury's revenue scale, growth rate, and unit economics relative to its peer set.
